segments.text: What up, I guess.
Braxton, I'm really excited for this conversation, man.
This is a big moment.
All right.
We're officially extending you the full-time off.
Let's go.
This is really an expression
of how much the LARP team values you
and the growth you've shown during your internship journey.
Thanks, man.
Take your time, but I'll just say,
we're incredibly proud, right?
And we have no doubt that you're ready
to be a full-time contributor within the organization.
Wait, I think there might be a mistake.
It says it's the same pay.
Right.
It's structured a little differently,
but it's really about increased ownership
and an improved title that reflects your newly expanded scope.
But I'm still hourly at the same rate?
That's chopped, bro.
Actually, it's very standard, right?
A lot of high-growth organizations
are moving in the same direction.
Are they?
Absolutely.
And I'll remind you at LARP,
your long-term earning potential is still very substantial,
right?
So this is really just about
getting you into the system in a bigger way.
Bro.
We're honestly thrilled to have you
as a part of our family, right?
And selfishly, I'm really excited
to see you take up more space in our daily forums.
Yeah.
I don't know.
What do you mean?
I mean, not to sound arrogant,
but I basically kept your platform afloat
for the past year,
and y'all can't even bump my pay?
I hear you, and I want to validate that feeling,
but compensation isn't always the best indicator
of value at this stage, right?
Remember, we're giving you an opportunity here
to really unlock a higher-impact version of yourself
within the org, right?
What?
It's about trajectory, right?
And you're on a really strong trajectory here, my friend.
I think I just need a second to process this.
Of course, but I just want to make sure we're aligned.
This is what you wanted, right?
It's what I thought I wanted.
Right.
Well, I'm confident you'll make the right decision.
Yeah, I'll let you know.
You mean circle back.
I'll let you know.
